prose:
Method–provenance block:
Property: dipole moment (|µ|) in Debye.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in xtb. Density-fitting is intrinsic to the method (no Gaussian basis).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/electrostatics-validation/methylamine/methylamine.xyz; single-point electrostatics calculation, no geometry relaxation (GFN2-xTB//input-xyz).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb backend (version not echoed in JSON); chemkit electrostatics skill.
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged and integrity check passed (partial charges sum to total charge). No warnings were issued.
Live log & files: engine stdout/stderr streamed to electrostatics_<timestamp>.out in the working directory while running; result JSON saved at /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpu9bpq2dx/live.json.

Headline result
Dipole moment = 1.4945174 D

This number is a computed value (GFN2-xTB) on a fixed geometry; accuracy is in the semi-empirical regime and should not be taken as a high-level ab-initio benchmark without further validation.
